name: kognitos-bdk-api
documentation:
# Python API for Book Development Kit
 
This package serves as a Python API tailored for the creation of books.

modules.name: kognitos.bdk.reflection.types.scalar
documentation: pylint: disable=invalid-name

modules.name: kognitos.bdk.reflection.factory.attrs_utils
documentation: This module is meant to be used only if attrs library is installed. The library is an opt-in dependency.

classes.name: kognitos.bdk.errors.SignatureError
documentation:
Raised by BDK's type checkers when there is a mismatch between the english signature and the
python signature of a procedure

modules.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints
documentation: Home for all Blueprint classes.

modules.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint
documentation: type: ignore [reportReturnType]

classes.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileReference
documentation: Contains all information required to identify a file within a storage system

classes.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FolderReference
documentation: Contains all information required to identify a folder within a storage system

classes.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.get_root_folder
documentation:
Gets a reference to the root folder.

Returns:
    a folder reference

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.get_folder
documentation:
Gets a reference to a folder.

Input Concepts:
    the path: The path to the folder

Returns:
    a folder reference

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.list_items
documentation:
Lists items from a folder reference

Input Concepts:
    the folder: The folder reference from which to list the items
    the filter expression: A filter expression to filter the items in the folder

Returns:
    a list of Items (file or folder) containing the items in the folder

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.rename_item
documentation:
Rename an item (file or folder) to a given name

Input Concepts:
    the item: The item (file or folder) to rename
    the name: The new name of the item
    the conflict behavior: The behavior to use on conflict scenarios. It should be one of the following values: ('fail', 'replace', 'rename')

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.delete_an_item
documentation:
Delete an item (file or folder)

Input Concepts:
    the item: The item (file or folder) to delete

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.download_item
documentation:
Download a file

Input Concepts:
    the file: The file reference to the file to download

Returns:
    the file as an IO object

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.create_folder
documentation:
Create a (folder) in another folder

Input Concepts:
    the another folder: The folder to create the (folder) in
    the folder name: The name of the (folder) to create
    the conflict behavior: The behavior to use on conflict scenarios. It should be one of the following values: ('fail', 'replace', 'rename')

Returns:
    a folder reference to the created folder

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.upload_file_to_folder
documentation:
Upload a file to a folder

Input Concepts:
    the file: The file to upload
    the folder: The folder to upload the file to
    the file name: The name of the file to upload
    the conflict behavior: The behavior to use on conflict scenarios. It should be one of the following values: ('fail', 'replace', 'rename')

Returns:
    a file reference to the uploaded file

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.copy_item
documentation:
Copy an item to a folder

Input Concepts:
    the item: The item (file or folder) to copy
    the folder: The folder to copy the item to
    the conflict behavior: The behavior to use on conflict scenarios. It should be one of the following values: ('fail', 'replace', 'rename')

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.blueprints.file_storage_blueprint.FileStorageBlueprint.move_item
documentation:
Move an item to a folder

Input Concepts:
    the item: The item (file or folder) to move
    the folder: The folder to move the item to
    the conflict behavior: The behavior to use on conflict scenarios. It should be one of the following values: ('fail', 'replace', 'rename')
A blueprint for files.

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.docstring.error.DocstringParseError.__init__
documentation: Initialize the exception with an optional error message.

type.name: str | int | bool | float | date | datetime | time

methods.name: kognitos.bdk.decorators.oauth_decorator.oauth.__init__
documentation: pylint: disable=too-many-arguments

classes.name: kognitos.bdk.api.noun_phrase.NounPhrase
documentation:
Represents a noun phrase.

Args:
    head (str): The head of the noun phrase.
    modifiers (List[str], optional): The modifiers of the noun phrase. Defaults to None.

Methods:
    __str__(): Returns a string representation of the noun phrase.
    to_string(): Returns a string representation of the noun phrase.

Examples:
    # Create a noun phrase with the head "cat"
    >>> np = NounPhrase("cat")
    >>> print(np)
    cat

    # Create a noun phrase with the head "dog" and modifiers ["big", "white"]
    >>> np = NounPhrase("dog", ["big", "white"])
    >>> print(np)
    big white dog

functions.name: kognitos.bdk.decorators.book_decorator.book
documentation:
kwargs:
    id: Optional[str] The book identifier
    icon: Optional[str] Path to the book icon
    name: Optional[str] The book name. If not provided, the name will be inferred from the class name.
    noun_phrase: Optional[str] The book noun phrase
